title: Get one-way flights for as low as $59 on Southwest for a limited time
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Southwest Airlines offers one-way flights starting at $59 in a four-day sale ending May 14, 11:59 p.m. PT.
Deals available for travel from June 2 to Nov. 18 domestically and Aug. 11 to Nov. 18 internationally.
Discounted flights from Boston include $59 to Baltimore and $197 to Atlanta; bookings require 21-day advance purchase.

title: Parts of Storrow Drive, Soldiers Field Road to face nightly closures
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Nightly closures on Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road will begin, spanning 8 p.m. to 5 a.m., through August.
Closure stretches from North Harvard Street to Mugar Way in Boston, impacting both eastbound and westbound traffic.
Detours via Anderson and Memorial Drive are advised; work tied to Storrow Drive tunnel maintenance project phase one.

title: Self-serve ticket machine yields $10 million winner at Boston supermarket
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rock Solid Trust claimed a $10 million lottery prize from a ticket bought at Stop & Shop in Dorchester.
The trust, represented by Joshua Garick, opted for a $6.5 million cash payment before taxes.
Stop & Shop at 545 Freeport St. receives a $50,000 bonus for selling the winning ticket.

title: Equestrian retailer Dover Saddlery warns of more than 100 potential layoffs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Dover Saddlery may lay off 112 employees at its Littleton, MA facility following potential business closure or sale.
The company's future depends on securing funds or selling, affecting 37 stores nationwide, details unclear.
Promus Equity Partners acquired Dover Saddlery in 2022; the company is seeking additional investments and franchise opportunities.

title: Officials: Newly opened Mass Pike ramp will decrease traffic congestion
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The new Mass Pike ramp connecting to I-495 opened, aiming to reduce congestion and improve safety in Central Massachusetts.
The updated interchange provides improved acceleration and deceleration lanes for over 100,000 daily vehicles on I-90 and I-495.
MassDOT advises caution with new signage as drivers adjust, especially with upcoming events like Foxborough's World Cup matches.

title: Red Sox could be in range to pick Mass. standout Brody Bumila in MLB Draft
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Brody Bumila, a 6-foot-9 left-hander from Bishop Feehan, recently threw a 20-strikeout no-hitter reaching 100 mph.
Bumila is projected as a first-round pick in the 2026 MLB Draft, which runs from July 11-13.
MLB mock drafts have Bumila going near the Red Sox's 20th pick, with projections placing him between 18th and 21st.

title: U.S. Honor Flag to be deployed to Mass. for fallen trooper’s memorial
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: U.S. Honor Flag will be delivered to Massachusetts for memorials honoring fallen State Trooper Kevin Trainor.
Trainor, 30, was killed by a wrong-way driver on Route 1 in Lynnfield, serving as a state trooper for three years.
Memorial services include a Tuesday wake and Wednesday funeral in Salem, with expected road closures and tributes.

title: Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brookline voters approved a tax override to prevent cuts to public safety jobs and school positions, passing with 8,859 to 5,826 votes.
The tax override will generate $23 million more in revenue, with $18 million allocated to public schools and $5 million to the town.
Brookline set a record 35% turnout for the local election, approving the override to increase property taxes by 18% over three years.

title: Do the Bruins have a potential No. 1 center already on their roster?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins aim to fill No. 1 center role amid retooling efforts after Bergeron and Krejci's retirements in 2023.
Young forwards Fraser Minten and James Hagens may develop into potential top-line centers for Boston.
Trades for established centers remain challenging due to high competition and price in the NHL market.

title: Maine police arrest victim’s boyfriend for 1987 murder
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Maine police arrested Stephen Bouchard, 63, charging him with the 1987 murder of Alice Hawkes in Westbrook.
Bouchard and Hawkes were living together when Hawkes was found dead in their apartment on October 4, 1987.
A 2025 reinvestigation by Maine's Major Crimes Unit led to the decision to charge Bouchard with murder.
